mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the equity discount rate and eases financial conditions, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days when policy is moving down from recent highs.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is accelerating while FEDFUNDS stays low, real rates fall and investors expect future tightening; that combination should pressure equity multiples and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above FEDFUNDS by at least 1.0pp
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show negative 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the CPI-minus-FEDFUNDS spread has no negative conditional return impact in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals slowing household income growth and weaker demand, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 20 trading days when unemployment is trending higher.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not negative in most unemployment-upturn episodes, or if the signal loses explanatory power after controlling for the market's own recent trend.

mechanism_narrative: A broad improvement in retail sales, housing starts, and payrolls indicates stronger domestic demand that lifts revenue expectations and should raise S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if jointly positive changes in RSAFS, HOUST, and PAYEMS do not precede higher 60-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal is weaker than each single series alone.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in INDPRO together with stable or falling CPIAUCSL implies real output is rising faster than inflation, which supports margins and should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change is positive and CPIAUCSL 3-month change is not above 0.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the conditional 20-day forward return is not positive when production improves without inflation acceleration, or if the interaction term becomes insignificant across two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A slowdown in GDPC1 growth signals weakening aggregate demand and lower earnings revisions, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 60 trading days when quarterly real GDP decelerates.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly annualized growth falls below 1.5%
failure_condition: Reject if low-GDP-growth quarters are not followed by below-median 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign flips positive in more than half of recessionary slowdowns.

mechanism_narrative: Stable PAYEMS with low UNRATE indicates a resilient labor market that sustains consumption and corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is at or below 4.0% and PAYEMS is not falling month over month
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-unemployment and stable-payroll condition does not precede positive 5-day SPX returns more often than baseline, or if the interaction coefficient is unstable across rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During a tightening cycle, an elevated FEDFUNDS rate combined with rising CPIAUCSL increases real financing costs and compresses valuation multiples, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation is positive
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate, positive-inflation periods do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the conditional hit rate falls below 40% across multiple tightening episodes.
